category_title: Children with social and communication difficulties including autism
minimal_context: The student is sitting in a circle with the class for a philosophical inquiry session and is expected to listen to a stimulus text and wait for their turn to speak.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are starting our philosophical inquiry session. I will read the text, and then we will take turns sharing our thoughts with the group.

STUDENT: (Raises hand immediately before the text is finished) I want to say something right now!

TUTOR: Please wait your turn, we are still listening to the story.

STUDENT: (Drops hand and turns to the student sitting next to him in the circle) Hey, look at what I can do with my shoes.

TUTOR: Let's try to stay on task and focus on the discussion.

STUDENT: (Interrupts the group discussion loudly) This is taking too long! Watch this, everyone! (Starts making noises and waving his arms to get the attention of the other children in the circle)

options.A: Ask the student to make contemporary connections to historical events related to the stimulus text during the group discussion.
options.B: Introduce poems aimed at promoting reflection and reinforcing mindfulness concepts during the philosophical inquiry session with the group.
options.C: Edit the stimulus text on the slides projected to the group, adding sound effects and motion features to the presentation.
options.D: Require participants to agree or disagree with at least one previous contribution and provide reasons, prohibiting the use of technical language or jargon.
